title: gh action: fix git-validation workflow
body:
In general if we hand a range like 'HEAD\~2..HEAD' to git it fails if the
repo only has two commits as HEAD\~2 doesn't resolve in such case.
git-validation doesn't seem to fail in such case but it still misses the
first commit.

To fix this we already checked out all PR commits anyway so we can just
validate all commits. But because there is special gh action handling in
git-validation we must disable it as it seem to only check the last
commit based from the GITHUB_ENV var.

comments.body:
I tested that in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/actions/runs/17297367515/job/49098874950?pr=288 and it seem to work correct now.

@mtrmac You were right that the current version didn't work properly, can you test this one with a merge commit example?
IIRC `git-validation` is somewhat slow with a large number of commits. I’ll try to both set up a reproducer, and look for a better way, sometime next week.

> @Luap99 Do I understand you correctly that you have found that the current logic is incorrect even with a completely linear commit sequence?

yes it always skips the first commit in a PR, so for most single  commit PRs it didn't validate anything. 


> IIRC `git-validation` is somewhat slow with a large number of commits. I’ll try to both set up a reproducer, and look for a better way, sometime next week.

Oh I think my commit message was not clear, then, the checkout action is configured to only checkout the last X commit where X is the PR commit number. As such the git-validation run only has a few PR commits to check.
